stages.name: The Sephiroth
stages.description: The 10 spheres of the Tree of Life. Master these and every minor pip's home is yours.

stages.name: The Hebrew Letters
stages.description: The 22 letters that carry the Major Arcana paths. The alphabet of heaven.

stages.name: The 36 Decans
stages.description: The wheel of the zodiac, divided 12 times 3, mapped to 36 minor pips. The calendar of the soul.

stages.name: The Court
stages.description: The 16 court cards as the 4×4 element-of-element matrix. Royalty as elemental position.

stages.name: The Decode Pass
stages.description: All 78 cards, one per day, integrating everything. Build your own Book T from inside out.

lessons.title: The Tree of Life
lessons.intro: Before any letter, before any card, there is the Tree. Etz Chaim. Ten spheres (sephiroth) connected by twenty-two paths. This is the diagram on which the entire Western esoteric tradition is built. The Hermetic Tarot is not a deck of pictures — it is a map of this Tree. The 78 cards are the 78 positions on it: 40 minor pips for the sephiroth in four worlds, 16 court cards for the elemental rulers, 22 majors for the paths. Today you meet the Tree itself. Tomorrow, the first sephirah.

data.stage_overview: Stage 1 covers all 10 sephiroth — the spheres of the Tree. By the end you will know which sephirah every minor card lives in, simply by reading its number.

quiz.question: How many sephiroth are on the Tree of Life?
quiz.explanation: Ten spheres. Plus 22 paths between them = 32 total positions, often called the '32 paths of wisdom' in Sefer Yetzirah.

quiz.question: How many paths connect the sephiroth?
quiz.explanation: Twenty-two paths, one for each Hebrew letter, one for each Major Arcana card.

quiz.question: How many cards in a Tarot deck does this account for?
quiz.explanation: 40 minor pips (10 sephiroth × 4 suits) + 16 courts + 22 majors = 78.

lessons.reflection: Look at the Tree image on this page. Where does your eye go first? Whatever sphere catches you, that's an early signal of your relationship to it.

lessons.title: 1. Kether — Crown
lessons.intro: Kether is the Crown. The first emanation. The singular point from which all else flows. In the Tarot, Kether is where the four Aces live — 'the Root of the Powers of Fire/Water/Air/Earth.' Pure potential, prior to form, prior to division. Above Kether is only Ain Soph Aur, the Limitless Light.

quiz.question: What is the planetary attribution of Kether?
quiz.explanation: Kether (Crown) is attributed to Primum Mobile.

quiz.question: What rank of pip cards lives in Kether?
quiz.explanation: Sephirah 1 → all four Aces of the deck (one per suit/world).

quiz.question: What is the Hebrew name?
quiz.explanation: Kether = כתר in Hebrew, meaning 'Crown'.

lessons.reflection: Pull all four Aces aside. Hold them. They are all Kether — the same singular point, expressed through four lenses.

lessons.title: 2. Chokmah — Wisdom
lessons.intro: Chokmah is Wisdom. The first masculine emanation. The Father. Pure outpouring force. In the Tarot, the four Twos sit here — every Two is a primal direction taken, a first move from the singularity. Chokmah is attributed to the Zodiac itself (Mazloth) — the field of all astrological possibility before any specific configuration.

quiz.question: What is the planetary attribution of Chokmah?
quiz.explanation: Chokmah (Wisdom) is attributed to Zodiac (Mazloth).

quiz.question: What rank of pip cards lives in Chokmah?
quiz.explanation: Sephirah 2 → all four 2s of the deck (one per suit/world).

quiz.question: What is the Hebrew name?
quiz.explanation: Chokmah = חכמה in Hebrew, meaning 'Wisdom'.

lessons.reflection: Pull the four Twos. Sit with them. Every Two is a 'first move from the One.' What did each suit choose to do first?

lessons.title: 3. Binah — Understanding
lessons.intro: Binah is Understanding. The first feminine emanation. The Mother. The womb that receives Chokmah's force and gives it form. The four Threes live here. Binah is attributed to Saturn (Shabbathai) — the great limiter, the giver of structure, time, and death. Without Binah's restriction there is no manifestation.

quiz.question: What is the planetary attribution of Binah?
quiz.explanation: Binah (Understanding) is attributed to Saturn (Shabbathai).

quiz.question: What rank of pip cards lives in Binah?
quiz.explanation: Sephirah 3 → all four 3s of the deck (one per suit/world).

quiz.question: What is the Hebrew name?
quiz.explanation: Binah = בינה in Hebrew, meaning 'Understanding'.

lessons.reflection: Pull the four Threes. They are all the Mother forming the Father's force. Notice the gravity of structure in each.

lessons.title: 4. Chesed — Mercy
lessons.intro: Chesed is Mercy. The first sephirah below the Abyss. Expansive, generous, cosmic order. The four Fours sit here — every Four is consolidation, structure built, sovereignty established. Chesed is Jupiter (Tzedek) — abundance, expansion, kingship. The benevolent law-giver.

quiz.question: What is the planetary attribution of Chesed?
quiz.explanation: Chesed (Mercy) is attributed to Jupiter (Tzedek).

quiz.question: What rank of pip cards lives in Chesed?
quiz.explanation: Sephirah 4 → all four 4s of the deck (one per suit/world).

quiz.question: What is the Hebrew name?
quiz.explanation: Chesed = חסד in Hebrew, meaning 'Mercy'.

lessons.reflection: Pull the four Fours. Each is a kingdom established. Where in your life have you built a Four?

lessons.title: 5. Geburah — Severity
lessons.intro: Geburah is Severity. Mars (Madim). The destroyer of what should not stand. The four Fives live here, and notice — every Five is harsh: Strife, Loss in Pleasure, Defeat, Material Trouble. This is not because Geburah is evil. It is because limitation cuts. Geburah is the surgeon's blade. Without it, everything bloats.

quiz.question: What is the planetary attribution of Geburah?
quiz.explanation: Geburah (Severity) is attributed to Mars (Madim).

quiz.question: What rank of pip cards lives in Geburah?
quiz.explanation: Sephirah 5 → all four 5s of the deck (one per suit/world).

quiz.question: What is the Hebrew name?
quiz.explanation: Geburah = גבורה in Hebrew, meaning 'Severity'.

lessons.reflection: Pull the four Fives. They are uncomfortable on purpose. What needed cutting in your life that hurt to lose?

lessons.title: 6. Tiphareth — Beauty
lessons.intro: Tiphareth is Beauty. The Sun (Shemesh). The center of the Tree, the heart, the Christ-point, the place where the Supernal flows down and the manifest reaches up. The four Sixes sit here, and they are all gentle: Victory, Pleasure, Earned Success, Material Success. Tiphareth balances. The card of the path leading here is The Sun. Memorize this center first.

quiz.question: What is the planetary attribution of Tiphareth?
quiz.explanation: Tiphareth (Beauty) is attributed to Sun (Shemesh).

quiz.question: What rank of pip cards lives in Tiphareth?
quiz.explanation: Sephirah 6 → all four 6s of the deck (one per suit/world).

quiz.question: What is the Hebrew name?
quiz.explanation: Tiphareth = תפארת in Hebrew, meaning 'Beauty'.

lessons.reflection: Pull the four Sixes. Sit with the Sun in each suit. This is where the Tree's gravity centers. Breathe.

lessons.title: 7. Netzach — Victory
lessons.intro: Netzach is Victory. Venus (Nogah). The sphere of feeling, attraction, desire, art. The four Sevens live here, and notice their flavor: Valour, Illusionary Success, Unstable Effort, Success Unfulfilled. Netzach is impassioned but not yet rational — the heart pulled toward the beautiful, sometimes deceived by what merely glitters.

quiz.question: What is the planetary attribution of Netzach?
quiz.explanation: Netzach (Victory) is attributed to Venus (Nogah).

quiz.question: What rank of pip cards lives in Netzach?
quiz.explanation: Sephirah 7 → all four 7s of the deck (one per suit/world).

quiz.question: What is the Hebrew name?
quiz.explanation: Netzach = נצח in Hebrew, meaning 'Victory'.

lessons.reflection: Pull the four Sevens. The pull of beauty. The risk of illusion. Where does desire mislead you?

lessons.title: 8. Hod — Splendour
lessons.intro: Hod is Splendour. Mercury (Kokab). The sphere of intellect, language, logic, ritual form. The four Eights sit here: Swiftness, Abandoned Success, Shortened Force, Prudence. Hod weighs and measures. It is the brain to Netzach's heart. Magicians work most directly through Hod — the structured invocation, the named correspondence.

quiz.question: What is the planetary attribution of Hod?
quiz.explanation: Hod (Splendour) is attributed to Mercury (Kokab).

quiz.question: What rank of pip cards lives in Hod?
quiz.explanation: Sephirah 8 → all four 8s of the deck (one per suit/world).

quiz.question: What is the Hebrew name?
quiz.explanation: Hod = הוד in Hebrew, meaning 'Splendour'.

lessons.reflection: Pull the four Eights. Where do you over-think? Where does Hod serve and where does it stall?

lessons.title: 9. Yesod — Foundation
lessons.intro: Yesod is Foundation. The Moon (Levanah). The astral plane. The sphere where all images take their final shape before manifesting in Malkuth. The four Nines live here: Great Strength, Material Happiness, Despair and Cruelty, Material Gain. Yesod is the interface between mind and matter. Dream, vision, sex, the unconscious — all Yesod.

quiz.question: What is the planetary attribution of Yesod?
quiz.explanation: Yesod (Foundation) is attributed to Moon (Levanah).

quiz.question: What rank of pip cards lives in Yesod?
quiz.explanation: Sephirah 9 → all four 9s of the deck (one per suit/world).

quiz.question: What is the Hebrew name?
quiz.explanation: Yesod = יסוד in Hebrew, meaning 'Foundation'.

lessons.reflection: Pull the four Nines. They are dreams crystallizing. What's currently in your astral foundation, taking shape?

lessons.title: 10. Malkuth — Kingdom
lessons.intro: Malkuth is the Kingdom. The Earth itself. The four elements. The body. The four Tens sit here: Oppression, Perfected Success, Ruin, Wealth. Malkuth receives everything from above and renders it physical. The bottom of the Tree is also where the Princesses (Earth-of-each-suit) hold their thrones. Reality is here.

quiz.question: What is the planetary attribution of Malkuth?
quiz.explanation: Malkuth (Kingdom) is attributed to Earth (Olam ha-Yesodoth).

quiz.question: What rank of pip cards lives in Malkuth?
quiz.explanation: Sephirah 10 → all four 10s of the deck (one per suit/world).

quiz.question: What is the Hebrew name?
quiz.explanation: Malkuth = מלכות in Hebrew, meaning 'Kingdom'.

lessons.reflection: Pull the four Tens. Notice both the heaviness (Oppression, Ruin) and the fullness (Success, Wealth). Malkuth holds both.

lessons.title: Stage 1 Review — The Ten Spheres
lessons.intro: You've met all ten sephiroth. This review tests integration. The exam is short but each question requires you to hold the whole Tree in mind at once. After this, you will not look at a minor card the same way — its number IS its sephirah, and that single fact unlocks half the deck.

quiz.question: Which sephirah holds the four Sixes?
quiz.explanation: Tiphareth (Sun, Beauty) sits at #6. All four Sixes are 'gentle wins' because Tiphareth balances.

quiz.question: Where does every Five live?
quiz.explanation: Geburah (Mars, Severity) at #5. The harsh Fives reflect Geburah's cutting nature.

quiz.question: What's the planetary attribution of the sephirah for the Tens?
quiz.explanation: Tens live in Malkuth (Kingdom). Malkuth is Earth/Elements (Olam ha-Yesodoth).

quiz.question: Which sephirah is the Moon?
quiz.explanation: Yesod (Foundation, Moon, Levanah) — the astral plane, where forms gather before manifesting in Malkuth.

quiz.question: Aces represent what kind of reality?
quiz.explanation: All Aces sit in Kether — the singular emanation, prior to form.

lessons.reflection: Pull a random pip card. Without looking at attributions, name its sephirah from memory. Then look. If you got it right, that's mastery beginning.

lessons.title: The Alphabet of Heaven
lessons.intro: The 22 Hebrew letters are the structural skeleton of the Major Arcana. Each letter is a path on the Tree of Life. Each path connects two sephiroth. Each carries a planet, a zodiac sign, or an element. Sefer Yetzirah, the oldest Hermetic text, divides the 22 into three groups: 3 mothers (the elements), 7 doubles (the planets), and 12 simples (the zodiac). This taxonomy IS the structure of reality, in this tradition. Learn the letters, and you have learned the system.

quiz.question: How many Hebrew letters in the alphabet?
quiz.explanation: Twenty-two — matching the 22 Majors and the 22 paths on the Tree.

quiz.question: How many 'mother' letters carry the elements?
quiz.explanation: Aleph (Air), Mem (Water), Shin (Fire). Three mothers.

quiz.question: How many 'double' letters carry the planets?
quiz.explanation: Seven planets in the classical system: Mercury, Moon, Venus, Jupiter, Mars, Sun, Saturn (Beth, Gimel, Daleth, Kaph, Pe, Resh, Tav).

lessons.reflection: Notice the math: 3 mothers + 7 doubles + 12 simples = 22 = paths = Major Arcana. The number is not arbitrary. Sit with that.

lessons.title: Path 11 — Aleph (א) → Fool
lessons.intro: Aleph is the first letter, but also a 'mother letter.' Three letters in Hebrew (Aleph, Mem, Shin) carry the elements (Air, Water, Fire). Aleph is Air — the breath that animates. Its path is the first connecting Kether to Chokmah. Its card is The Fool — the empty step, undivided potential, before form.

quiz.question: Which Major Arcana card carries the path of Aleph?
quiz.explanation: Aleph (א) carries the Fool on path 11, attribution: Air.

quiz.question: What is the attribution of Aleph?
quiz.explanation: Aleph = Air (mother letter).

quiz.question: Is Aleph a mother, double, or simple letter?
quiz.explanation: Aleph is a mother letter. Carries an element.

lessons.reflection: Find the Fool in your Dowson deck. Look at the card's printed Hebrew letter. It is Aleph (א). See it. Now you can read this layer of every Major.

lessons.title: Path 12 — Beth (ב) → Magus
lessons.intro: Beth means 'house.' One of the seven 'double letters' (each carries a planet). Beth is Mercury — the swift messenger, the magician's intelligence. Its path goes from Kether to Binah. Its card is The Magus — pure articulating intellect. The first action that follows the Fool's leap.

quiz.question: Which Major Arcana card carries the path of Beth?
quiz.explanation: Beth (ב) carries the Magus on path 12, attribution: Mercury.

quiz.question: What is the attribution of Beth?
quiz.explanation: Beth = Mercury (double letter).

quiz.question: Is Beth a mother, double, or simple letter?
quiz.explanation: Beth is a double letter. Carries a planet.

lessons.reflection: Find the Magus in your Dowson deck. Look at the card's printed Hebrew letter. It is Beth (ב). See it. Now you can read this layer of every Major.

lessons.title: Path 13 — Gimel (ג) → High Priestess
lessons.intro: Gimel means 'camel' — the beast that crosses the desert. Double letter, Moon. Its path is the longest of all: Kether straight down to Tiphareth, crossing the Abyss. The High Priestess walks this. She is the lunar bridge between the Crown and the Heart. The keeper of the secret crossing.

quiz.question: Which Major Arcana card carries the path of Gimel?
quiz.explanation: Gimel (ג) carries the High Priestess on path 13, attribution: Moon.

quiz.question: What is the attribution of Gimel?
quiz.explanation: Gimel = Moon (double letter).

quiz.question: Is Gimel a mother, double, or simple letter?
quiz.explanation: Gimel is a double letter. Carries a planet.

lessons.reflection: Find the High Priestess in your Dowson deck. Look at the card's printed Hebrew letter. It is Gimel (ג). See it. Now you can read this layer of every Major.

lessons.title: Path 14 — Daleth (ד) → Empress
lessons.intro: Daleth is 'door.' Double letter, Venus. Its path runs across the top of the Tree: Chokmah to Binah, Father to Mother. Its card is The Empress — the generative threshold, fertility, the door through which form enters. Beauty as the principle by which one sphere births another.

quiz.question: Which Major Arcana card carries the path of Daleth?
quiz.explanation: Daleth (ד) carries the Empress on path 14, attribution: Venus.

quiz.question: What is the attribution of Daleth?
quiz.explanation: Daleth = Venus (double letter).

quiz.question: Is Daleth a mother, double, or simple letter?
quiz.explanation: Daleth is a double letter. Carries a planet.

lessons.reflection: Find the Empress in your Dowson deck. Look at the card's printed Hebrew letter. It is Daleth (ד). See it. Now you can read this layer of every Major.

lessons.title: Path 15 — He (ה) → Emperor
lessons.intro: He is a simple letter, attributed to Aries — the first sign of the zodiac, fire's first ignition. Its path runs Chokmah to Tiphareth. Its card is The Emperor — sovereign authority, the structure that contains the spring fire. (Crowley's Book of the Law swaps He and Tzaddi; this curriculum stays Golden Dawn orthodox.)

quiz.question: Which Major Arcana card carries the path of He?
quiz.explanation: He (ה) carries the Emperor on path 15, attribution: Aries.

quiz.question: What is the attribution of He?
quiz.explanation: He = Aries (simple letter).

quiz.question: Is He a mother, double, or simple letter?
quiz.explanation: He is a simple letter. Carries a zodiac sign.

lessons.reflection: Find the Emperor in your Dowson deck. Look at the card's printed Hebrew letter. It is He (ה). See it. Now you can read this layer of every Major.

lessons.title: Path 16 — Vau (ו) → Hierophant
lessons.intro: Vau is 'nail' — the joiner. Simple letter, Taurus, the bull. Its path runs Chokmah to Chesed. Its card is The Hierophant — the teacher, the joiner of inner to outer, of student to lineage. Stable, slow, durable.

quiz.question: Which Major Arcana card carries the path of Vau?
quiz.explanation: Vau (ו) carries the Hierophant on path 16, attribution: Taurus.

quiz.question: What is the attribution of Vau?
quiz.explanation: Vau = Taurus (simple letter).

quiz.question: Is Vau a mother, double, or simple letter?
quiz.explanation: Vau is a simple letter. Carries a zodiac sign.

lessons.reflection: Find the Hierophant in your Dowson deck. Look at the card's printed Hebrew letter. It is Vau (ו). See it. Now you can read this layer of every Major.

lessons.title: Path 17 — Zayin (ז) → Lovers
lessons.intro: Zayin is 'sword.' Simple letter, Gemini — the twins, division and discernment. Its path runs Binah to Tiphareth. Its card is The Lovers — choice, the cutting decision between two paths. The sword is the weapon of the discriminating mind.

quiz.question: Which Major Arcana card carries the path of Zayin?
quiz.explanation: Zayin (ז) carries the Lovers on path 17, attribution: Gemini.

quiz.question: What is the attribution of Zayin?
quiz.explanation: Zayin = Gemini (simple letter).

quiz.question: Is Zayin a mother, double, or simple letter?
quiz.explanation: Zayin is a simple letter. Carries a zodiac sign.

lessons.reflection: Find the Lovers in your Dowson deck. Look at the card's printed Hebrew letter. It is Zayin (ז). See it. Now you can read this layer of every Major.

lessons.title: Path 18 — Cheth (ח) → Chariot
lessons.intro: Cheth is 'fence' — enclosure, vehicle. Simple letter, Cancer — the crab, the protective shell, the vehicle of the moon. Its path runs Binah to Geburah. Its card is The Chariot — disciplined movement within structure, will harnessed by armor.

quiz.question: Which Major Arcana card carries the path of Cheth?
quiz.explanation: Cheth (ח) carries the Chariot on path 18, attribution: Cancer.

quiz.question: What is the attribution of Cheth?
quiz.explanation: Cheth = Cancer (simple letter).

quiz.question: Is Cheth a mother, double, or simple letter?
quiz.explanation: Cheth is a simple letter. Carries a zodiac sign.

lessons.reflection: Find the Chariot in your Dowson deck. Look at the card's printed Hebrew letter. It is Cheth (ח). See it. Now you can read this layer of every Major.

lessons.title: Path 19 — Teth (ט) → Strength
lessons.intro: Teth is 'serpent.' Simple letter, Leo — the lion, but the serpent-power within the lion. Its path runs Chesed to Geburah, balancing mercy and severity. Its card is Strength — quiet courage, the woman with the lion. Note: GD orthodox places Strength at VIII (Leo), not XI.

quiz.question: Which Major Arcana card carries the path of Teth?
quiz.explanation: Teth (ט) carries the Strength on path 19, attribution: Leo.

quiz.question: What is the attribution of Teth?
quiz.explanation: Teth = Leo (simple letter).

quiz.question: Is Teth a mother, double, or simple letter?
quiz.explanation: Teth is a simple letter. Carries a zodiac sign.

lessons.reflection: Find the Strength in your Dowson deck. Look at the card's printed Hebrew letter. It is Teth (ט). See it. Now you can read this layer of every Major.

lessons.title: Path 20 — Yod (י) → Hermit
lessons.intro: Yod is the smallest letter, the seed-point of all the others. Simple, Virgo. Every Hebrew letter contains a Yod hidden in its first stroke. Its path runs Chesed to Tiphareth. Its card is The Hermit — solitary illumination, the seed-light withdrawing to find itself.

quiz.question: Which Major Arcana card carries the path of Yod?
quiz.explanation: Yod (י) carries the Hermit on path 20, attribution: Virgo.

quiz.question: What is the attribution of Yod?
quiz.explanation: Yod = Virgo (simple letter).

quiz.question: Is Yod a mother, double, or simple letter?
quiz.explanation: Yod is a simple letter. Carries a zodiac sign.

lessons.reflection: Find the Hermit in your Dowson deck. Look at the card's printed Hebrew letter. It is Yod (י). See it. Now you can read this layer of every Major.

lessons.title: Path 21 — Kaph (כ) → Wheel of Fortune
lessons.intro: Kaph is 'palm of the hand.' Double letter, Jupiter. Its path runs Chesed to Netzach. Its card is The Wheel of Fortune — Jupiter's expansive turning, the cosmic cycle. Fortune favors those who can read the wheel.

quiz.question: Which Major Arcana card carries the path of Kaph?
quiz.explanation: Kaph (כ) carries the Wheel of Fortune on path 21, attribution: Jupiter.

quiz.question: What is the attribution of Kaph?
quiz.explanation: Kaph = Jupiter (double letter).

quiz.question: Is Kaph a mother, double, or simple letter?
quiz.explanation: Kaph is a double letter. Carries a planet.

lessons.reflection: Find the Wheel of Fortune in your Dowson deck. Look at the card's printed Hebrew letter. It is Kaph (כ). See it. Now you can read this layer of every Major.

lessons.title: Path 22 — Lamed (ל) → Justice
lessons.intro: Lamed is 'ox-goad.' Simple letter, Libra — the scales of balance. Its path runs Geburah to Tiphareth. Its card is Justice (or in Thoth, Adjustment) — the corrective force, the precise sword that cuts only what must be cut. GD orthodox places Justice at XI.

quiz.question: Which Major Arcana card carries the path of Lamed?
quiz.explanation: Lamed (ל) carries the Justice on path 22, attribution: Libra.

quiz.question: What is the attribution of Lamed?
quiz.explanation: Lamed = Libra (simple letter).

quiz.question: Is Lamed a mother, double, or simple letter?
quiz.explanation: Lamed is a simple letter. Carries a zodiac sign.

lessons.reflection: Find the Justice in your Dowson deck. Look at the card's printed Hebrew letter. It is Lamed (ל). See it. Now you can read this layer of every Major.

lessons.title: Path 23 — Mem (מ) → Hanged Man
lessons.intro: Mem is 'water' — the second of the three mother letters. Element: Water. Its path runs Geburah to Hod. Its card is The Hanged Man — suspension, surrender, the drowning that becomes baptism. Initiatory inversion.

quiz.question: Which Major Arcana card carries the path of Mem?
quiz.explanation: Mem (מ) carries the Hanged Man on path 23, attribution: Water.

quiz.question: What is the attribution of Mem?
quiz.explanation: Mem = Water (mother letter).

quiz.question: Is Mem a mother, double, or simple letter?
quiz.explanation: Mem is a mother letter. Carries an element.

lessons.title: Path 32 — Tav (ת) → Universe

lessons.title: Stage 2 Review — The 22 Letters
lessons.intro: Twenty-two letters mastered. From here, every Major Arcana you draw will reveal its structural address: which letter, which path, which two sephiroth it connects. This is what RWS hides and what Hermetic decks broadcast. Test yourself against the structure.

quiz.question: Which letter is the first 'mother' (Air)?
quiz.explanation: Aleph (א) — the breath, Air, The Fool, path 11.

quiz.explanation: Resh (ר) — Sun, The Sun card, path 30.

quiz.explanation: Tav (ת) — Saturn (and Earth residual), The Universe, path 32.

lessons.reflection: Lay all 22 Majors face up in a line, in path order (Fool first, Universe last). You should be able to read off each one's letter and attribution without flipping it. If you can, Stage 2 is mastered.

lessons.title: The Wheel — 360°, Divided
lessons.intro: The zodiac is 360°. Each of the 12 signs occupies 30°. Each sign is divided into three decans of 10°, and each decan is ruled by a planet in Chaldean order. That gives 36 decans, and the Golden Dawn assigns each decan to one of the 36 minor pip cards (the 2 through 10 of each suit). This is why every minor card has a planet-in-sign attribution. Learn the 36 decans and you can name any pip's astrology on sight. The triplicity rule: fire signs (Aries/Leo/Sagittarius) hold Wands, water signs Cups, air signs Swords, earth signs Disks.

quiz.question: How many decans are in the zodiac?
quiz.explanation: 12 signs × 3 decans per sign = 36.

quiz.question: Each decan covers how many degrees?
quiz.explanation: 10° per decan. 30° per sign / 3 decans.

quiz.question: Which suit corresponds to fire signs?
quiz.explanation: Aries, Leo, Sagittarius (fire) → Wands. Water → Cups. Air → Swords. Earth → Disks.

lessons.reflection: Look at the Decan Wheel on this page. The outer ring shows the 12 signs. The inner ring shows the 36 decans with planetary rulers. This is the system you're learning to read.

lessons.title: ♈ Aries — Three Decans
lessons.intro: Aries is the first sign — fire's ignition, the cardinal initiating force. Its three decans rule the spring's first 30 days. They map to the three of Wands (the fire suit). Pay attention to the Mars-Sun-Venus sequence — this is the Chaldean order of planetary rulership applied within fire signs.

quiz.question: Which card is Mars in Aries (decan 1)?
quiz.explanation: Mars in Aries, decan 1 (0°-9°) → Two of Wands (Dominion).

quiz.question: Which card is Sun in Aries (decan 2)?
quiz.explanation: Sun in Aries, decan 2 (10°-19°) → Three of Wands (Established Strength).

quiz.question: Which card is Venus in Aries (decan 3)?
quiz.explanation: Venus in Aries, decan 3 (20°-29°) → Four of Wands (Perfected Work).

lessons.reflection: Pull the three Aries pip cards from your Dowson deck. Lay them in order (decan 1, 2, 3). Notice the planetary progression. Speak each: '[planet] in Aries, 0-30 degrees'.

lessons.title: ♉ Taurus — Three Decans
lessons.intro: Taurus is fixed earth — the bull, slow accumulation, sensual ground. The earth-suit (Disks) takes its first three pips here. Mercury-Moon-Saturn rule. Notice how earth's decans give us Material Trouble, Material Success, and Success Unfulfilled — the ambivalence of accumulating matter.

quiz.question: Which card is Mercury in Taurus (decan 1)?

lessons.reflection: Pull the three Taurus pip cards from your Dowson deck. Lay them in order (decan 1, 2, 3). Notice the planetary progression. Speak each: '[planet] in Taurus, 30-30 degrees'.

lessons.reflection: Shuffle the 36 minor pips. Pull one at random. Before reading the page, name its decan. Repeat ten times. Score yourself.

lessons.title: The Court — Element of Element
lessons.intro: The 16 court cards organize into a 4×4 grid: each suit (its element) crossed with a court rank (sub-element). King = Fire, Queen = Water, Prince = Air, Princess = Earth. So the King of Wands is 'Fire of Fire' (pure fiery fire). The Princess of Cups is 'Earth of Water' (water grounding into form). This element-of-element scheme is the Golden Dawn's most distinctive contribution and lets you read court personality from structure alone.

quiz.question: What element is the Queen rank?
quiz.explanation: Queen = Water across all four suits. Queen of Wands = Water of Fire, Queen of Cups = Water of Water, etc.

quiz.question: Which rank rules a quadrant of the heavens (no zodiacal degrees)?
quiz.explanation: Princesses are throne-bearers of the Aces. Earth fixes the suit's primal force.

quiz.question: What is the 'element of element' for the Prince of Cups?
quiz.explanation: Prince = Air. Cups = Water. Air of Water.

lessons.reflection: Pull all 16 courts. Lay them in a 4×4 grid: rows = suits, columns = ranks. You're looking at the entire elemental matrix of the GD system in one view.

lessons.title: King of Cups — Fire of Water
lessons.intro: King of Cups: Lord of the Waves and the Waters, King of the Hosts of the Sea. Element-of-element: Fire of Water. King = Fire of suit. Active, mounted, lance-bearing. The aggressive expression of the suit's element. Rules the last decan of one sign + first two decans of the next (a 30° span crossing a sign-boundary). Zodiacal range: Last decan Aquarius + first 2 decans Pisces (20° Aquarius - 20° Pisces).

Same 30° span structure. Zodiacal range: Last decan Libra + first 2 decans Scorpio (20° Libra - 20° Scorpio).

lessons.title: Stage 4 Review — The 16 Courts
lessons.intro: Sixteen courts mastered. The 4×4 elemental matrix is now a tool you can use, not a chart you have to look up. Court cards in readings stop being random personalities and start being precise elemental positions in a system you understand.

quiz.question: What is the element-of-element of the King of Cups?
quiz.explanation: King = Fire. Cups = Water. Fire of Water — the active expression of water.

quiz.question: Which court is 'Earth of Air'?
quiz.explanation: Princess = Earth. Swords = Air. Princess of Swords.

quiz.question: What rank rules the last decan of one sign + first two decans of the next?
quiz.explanation: All three of these ranks (NOT Princess) span 30° crossing a sign boundary.

quiz.question: What is the GD title of the Prince of Wands?
quiz.correct: Prince of the Chariot of Fire
quiz.explanation: Prince of the Chariot of Fire — the air-of-fire expression, charioted.

lessons.reflection: Without looking, write out the 4×4 court matrix from memory. Then check.

lessons.title: The Decode Pass — 78 Cards, One at a Time
lessons.intro: You have learned the system. Now you walk every card, one per day, integrating everything. Each card lesson brings together its sephirah, its world, its letter (if Major), its decan (if minor), its element-of-element (if court). This is where memorization becomes mastery. By Day 78 of this pass, you have built your own personal Book T from inside out.

quiz.question: How many cards in the deck?
quiz.explanation: 22 majors + 40 minors + 16 courts = 78.

quiz.question: What's the goal of this pass?
quiz.correct: Integrate all prior layers per card
quiz.explanation: Each card is a position holding sephirah + world + (letter or decan) + element. The pass turns memorized parts into integrated knowing.

lessons.reflection: This stage is paced over 78 days. One card per day. Don't rush. The deck rewards patience.

lessons.title: 1. The Fool
lessons.intro: The Fool. Path 11 on the Tree, carrying the Hebrew letter Aleph (א). Attribution: Air [mother]. Element: Air. Build the card mentally: see the Hebrew letter, feel its element/planet/sign, walk the path between its two sephiroth.

quiz.question: What Hebrew letter is on The Fool?
quiz.explanation: Aleph (א), path 11, attribution Air.

quiz.question: What is the path number?
quiz.explanation: Path 11 on the Tree, carrying Aleph.

lessons.reflection: Pull The Fool from your Dowson deck. Sit with it for at least 60 seconds. Speak its full attribution out loud. Tomorrow's card will be next in the pass.

lessons.title: Stage 5 Mastery — Walking the Deck
lessons.intro: Seventy-eight cards walked. You now have your own Book T, written from inside. The next phase is pathworking: using the 22 Major Arcana as scrying anchors and gates to walk the paths of the Tree. That phase is not paced lesson-by-lesson — it is a slow, ritual practice over months. The journey continues, but the foundation is laid.

quiz.question: What is The Hermit's Hebrew letter?
quiz.explanation: Yod (י) — Virgo, simple letter, path 20.

quiz.question: Which card is Saturn in Leo?
quiz.explanation: Saturn-Leo (1st decan Leo) → 5 of Wands ('Strife').

quiz.question: Which sephirah holds the 7 of Cups?
quiz.explanation: Sevens live in Netzach (Venus, Victory). 7 of Cups = Venus in Scorpio = 'Illusionary Success'.

quiz.question: What is the element-of-element of the Knight of Wands? (Note: GD calls this 'King of Wands.')
quiz.explanation: King (or Knight in Thoth) = Fire. Wands = Fire. Fire of Fire — the maximum-fire court.

lessons.reflection: The structural foundation is complete. Now: pathworking. Begin with path 32 (Tav, the Universe) — the first path the GD initiate walks. Sit with the card, build it mentally, step into it, journey, return. That's a different kind of practice. The deck is yours now.
